title: 10 best herbal creams for pimples

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to effectively combat acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which helps to reduce the severity and duration of pimples.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The herbal tea cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples, promoting a faster recovery and preventing further breakouts.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in herbal tea tree creams help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to pimple formation and exacerbate existing acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with pimples because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil extracted from its leaves has been shown to effectively combat acne-causing bacteria, such as P. acnes. It also reduces redness and inflammation associated with pimples. By soothing the skin and promoting a healthy balance of skin flora, tea tree oil cream can help to reduce the occurrence and severity of pimples, promoting clear and healthy-looking skin. This natural ingredient is a popular choice for acne treatment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps with pimples by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause acne.
Terpinen-4-ol: Contributes to the antiseptic and antibacterial properties of tea tree oil, which helps to prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria.
α-Terpineol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing redness and preventing the development of acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat acne and pimples.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their oil, which is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of pimples.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (or underground stems) of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to produce a concentrated extract that is rich in tea tree oil and other compounds with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity and occurrence of pimples.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in a glass container with 500 milliliters of carrier oil.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at a temperature of 60 to 70 degrees celsius.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 to 70 degrees celsius.
Pour the mixture into glass containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, allowing the skin to heal faster.

Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial agents inhibit the growth of bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which contributes to the development of acne and pimples, preventing infection and further inflammation.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and reducing scarring, helping to minimize pimple marks and discoloration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples. Its natural antiseptic properties combat the bacteria that cause acne, preventing further breakouts. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture and promote healthy skin, allowing the skin to heal faster and reducing the appearance of pimples and acne. This makes it an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These natural surfactants help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to the development of pimples.
Lupeol: A triterpenoid compound, lupeol has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal acne-prone skin, reducing the occurrence of pimples.
Vitamins A and C: These antioxidants help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of pimples and other skin issues.
remedy_parts: Gel from the Leaves: It has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and reduce acne.
Mucilages from the Leaves: They create a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further irritation.
Aloin from the Leaves: It has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to dry out pimples and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from 2 to 3 aloe vera leaves and let it sit for 30 minutes.
Weigh 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of shea butter and melt them together in a double boiler.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Combine the melted beeswax mixture with the aloe vera gel and mix well with an electric mixer.
Add the coconut oil and vitamin e mixture to the aloe vera mixture and stir until it thickens to a creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymoquinone and flavonoids, help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne and inflammation, thereby preventing the development of pimples.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in pot marigold cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, allowing for faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's rich antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to premature aging and exacerbating acne; antioxidants also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a smoother complexion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and saponins, reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Calendula's soothing and moisturizing effects also promote skin healing, reducing the appearance of pimples and preventing further irritation. Additionally, its natural antiseptic properties help prevent bacterial infections that can cause pimples, promoting healthy skin and a reduced risk of breakouts. This makes calendula cream a popular choice for acne treatment.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections that contribute to pimple formation, promoting a balanced skin environment.
Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and promoting healing of pimples.
Nashinone: This triterpenoid saponin has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to combat the underlying causes of pimples and promote healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Calendula flowers are commonly used in creams for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and reduce acne.
Leaves: Calendula leaves are also used, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help calm and protect the skin from irritation and damage.
Seeds: Calendula seeds contain saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that can aid in reducing inflammation and fighting bacterial infections that contribute to pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers when in full bloom for maximum potency and use 30g of dried flowers per 100g cream base.
Infuse 30g of dried calendula flowers in 200ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth to separate the plant material and discard the solids.
Combine the infused oil with 100g of cream base and 1 teaspoon of preservative like potassium sorbate.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and emulsifies into a smooth and consistent cream texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by pimples, providing quick relief from acne symptoms.

Antibacterial: Chamomile's antibacterial property inhibits the growth of bacteria that can cause pimples, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
Soothing and calming: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties help to calm red and irritated skin, reducing the appearance of acne scars and blemishes, and promoting a smoother complexion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Chamomile creams also have a calming effect on the skin, which can help to reduce stress-related breakouts and promote a healthy skin balance. Regular use of chamomile creams can lead to a reduction in pimple size and a decrease in their frequency.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation in the skin.

α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm and reduce inflammation associated with acne and pimples, promoting faster healing and minimizing scarring.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to calm and reduce the appearance of pimples.
Leaves: They are used for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help to combat acne-causing bacteria and promote healing.
Seeds: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce redness and irritation, and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them in a warm place for 2-3 days to preserve their potency.
Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infusion and let it cool down to room temperature within 30 minutes to an hour.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax with the cooled infusion in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to solidify and cool down completely before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea pallida contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antimicrobial properties: The plant's constituents, such as cinnamic acid and caffeic acid, exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria like Staphylococcus aureus (a common cause of acne), which can help prevent infections and reduce pimple severity.

Wound-healing and regenerative properties: KanSas coneflower contains saponins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids that may enhance wound healing and promote tissue regeneration, potentially helping to improve skin elasticity and reducing the appearance of acne scars.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyacetylenes, help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause acne. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia is believed to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to regulate the body's immune response and prevent future breakouts. As a result, Echinacea angustifolia creams can be a natural and effective solution for reducing pimple severity and promoting clearer skin.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to the formation of pimples.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
Triterpene Glycosides: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the appearance of pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for pimples due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acne and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in autumn after the first frost when the plant's sap has slowed.
Dry the harvested echinacea angustifolia roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Combine 10 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots with 10 grams of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Whip 2 tablespoons of shea butter with 2 tablespoons of olive oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to add a rich texture.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Geranium essential oil has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity, which helps combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that causes acne and leads to pimple formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, while also reducing inflammation that can lead to scarring.
Antioxidant properties: Geranium essential oil contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to the formation of acne and other skin issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pelargonium graveolens, also known as geranium, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The essential oil derived from this herb has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antimicrobial properties combat the growth of bacteria that can clog pores and lead to breakouts. Additionally, geranium oil's ability to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce stress can help regulate oil production, preventing clogged pores and promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples.
Limonene: This terpene has astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to dry out pimples and prevent bacterial growth, while also reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to the development of pimples, as well as prevent scarring and promote skin healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Pelargonium graveolens are used due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin.

Flowers: The flowers of Pelargonium graveolens are used because they contain essential oils that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in pimples.
Leaves (again, as stems and roots are less often used): The leaves are also used because they have astringent properties, which can help to reduce the size of pimples and prevent further breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and flowers from the garden on a sunny morning.
Infuse the harvested leaves and flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids after 4 weeks.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes then let it cool and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lavender's antibacterial property helps to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), and prevents further infection.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory property reduces redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with pimples, which helps to soothe and calm the skin.
Antioxidant property: Lavender's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The calming effects of lavender soothe the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. The antiseptic properties of lavender also help to combat bacterial infections that can lead to pimples. By promoting a healthy balance of skin flora and reducing inflammation, English lavender creams can effectively help to clear up pimples and prevent future breakouts, leaving skin looking clear and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that may help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause acne.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of pimples.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to the formation of pimples and acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: They are used due to their antibacterial properties, which help reduce the occurrence of acne-causing bacteria on the skin.
Stems: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn to preserve their aromatic properties and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 2 days to remove moisture.
Steep 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil at 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 10 minutes to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Neem cream's antibacterial properties help kill acne-causing bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Neem's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, making it an effective treatment for acne-prone skin.
Antioxidant properties: Neem cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, reducing the appearance of scars and hyperpigmentation caused by acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica, also known as neem, creams helps with pimples because of its potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as azadirachtin and nimbidin, have been shown to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause acne, thereby preventing the formation of pimples. Additionally, neem creams have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples, promoting a faster healing process and a clearer complexion. This makes neem creams an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Limonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help reduce redness and prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria.
Terpenoids (specially Azadirachtin): These compounds have potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help control acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with pimples.

Alkaloids (specially nimbin and nimandrin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness, prevent further inflammation, and protect the skin from oxidative damage associated with acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Azadirachta indica leaves are the most commonly used part to make creams for pimples due to their high neem oil content, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

Seeds: Neem seeds are used to create creams for pimples because they contain azadirachtin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce acne and prevent future breakouts.

Buds: Neem buds are used to make creams for pimples due to their high concentration of limonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried azadirachta indica leaves and 50g of dried azadirachta indica seeds.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Infuse 100g of distilled water with 50g of dried azadirachta indica leaves for 20 minutes.
Combine the infused water with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 5g of sweet almond oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Oats contain anti-inflammatory agents like avenanthramides and beta-glucans, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, soothing the skin and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbal extracts in oats creams, such as tea tree oil and chamomile, have antimicrobial effects that help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of pimples, reducing their growth and severity.
Soil binding and absorbency: Oat kernel's beta-glucans form a protective barrier on the skin, absorbing excess oil, dirt, and impurities, helping to unclog pores and prevent future breakouts.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oats, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's high content of saponins and avenanthramides helps to reduce redness and calm irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for acne-prone skin. Avena sativa creams also have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause pimples. Additionally, its moisturizing properties keep the skin hydrated and balanced, reducing the occurrence of pimples.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with pimples.
Saponins: These glycosides have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent bacterial infections and reduce inflammation that can lead to pimples.

Fatty acids (Oleic acid and Linoleic acid): These unsaturated fatty acids have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which help reduce inflammation, promote skin hydration, and prevent clogged pores that can lead to pimples.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make creams for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Stems: Used to make creams for pimples due to their soothing and calming effects on the skin.
Leaves: Used to make creams for pimples due to their astringent and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried avena sativa in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused avena sativa oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds to melt the beeswax.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean container and let it set at room temperature for 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Saw palmetto contains anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-carotene and lutein, which help reduce inflammation and redness associated with pimples.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in saw palmetto cream help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to pimple formation and worsening of acne symptoms.
Sebum-regulating activity: Saw palmetto has been shown to regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil that can clog pores and lead to pimples.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients work to reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, while also combating the bacterial infections that often cause them. The anti-inflammatory properties of Serenoa repens cream also help to calm and soothe the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and promoting overall skin health. This results in a clearer, more balanced complexion. Regular use can lead to improved skin clarity.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically saw palmetto iridoids, have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which contribute to pimple formation.

Fernane-type Sesquiterpenes: Fernane-type sesquiterpenes, such as serenoa sesquiterpene, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to pimple formation and severity.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help reduce oxidative stress and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to pimple formation and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin.
Leaves: They are used due to their content of saponins and other compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating pimples.
Barks: Although less common, barks are sometimes used due to their saponin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried serenoa repens powder and mix it with 300g of cold-pressed jojoba oil in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove any solids.
Add 5g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the mixture and heat it gently at 60-70°c for another 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, reduces inflammation that can cause acne by suppressing the activity of enzymes responsible for producing sebum, a natural oil that clogs pores. Additionally, turmeric's antibacterial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which contribute to pimple formation. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, turmeric creams provide effective relief from pimples, promoting clear and healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Turmeric's curcuminoids have antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that contributes to the development of acne.
Anti-inflammatory property: Curcumin in turmeric cream helps to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with pimples, which can lead to faster healing and reduced scarring.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, reduce oxidative stress, and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems, contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pimple inflammation and prevent scarring.
Roots: Turmeric roots have antibacterial properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing the severity of pimples and preventing further breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and dry 250 grams of curcuma longa powder in a clean glass container for 2 hours.
Mix 10 grams of dried curcuma longa powder with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping the mixture into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer.
Transfer the curdled cream to sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with pimples because they contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling. The antibacterial compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to eliminate acne-causing bacteria and prevent future breakouts. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with pimples, making it a natural and effective solution for acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Ginger's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to pimple formation, while its anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling.
Antibacterial Properties: Ginger's antibacterial compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, inhibit the growth of bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a common cause of acne.

Anti-Scarring and Skin-Soothing Properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help reduce post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) and scarring, while its soothing compounds calm the skin, reducing irritation and redness associated with pimples.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, rhyzomes help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Roots: Containing natural antibacterial properties, roots help combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root from your garden or purchase it from a local market weighing approximately one pound.
Grind the fresh ginger root into fine paste using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for about five minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the grated ginger paste with 10 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan over low heat for twenty-five minutes.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any impurities and transfer it to a clean glass jar for future use within seven days.
Combine one teaspoon of the filtered ginger extract with three tablespoons of beeswax and two tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler heating until melted then stirring well.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with pimples because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's ability to soothe and calm the skin reduces redness and irritation, allowing the pores to breathe and unclog naturally. Additionally, lemon balm has antimicrobial qualities that help combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing further breakouts and promoting a clear complexion. By using an herbal lemon balm cream, you can effectively treat and prevent pimples while nourishing your skin with its gentle, non-comedogenic properties.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lemon balm's antibacterial activity helps to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacterium that can contribute to acne and pimple formation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples, making them less noticeable and more manageable.
Antioxidant property: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to inflammation, which can contribute to the formation of acne lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Barks: Lemon balm bark has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as acne, due to its ability to reduce sebum production and fight against bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source or online supplier weighing approximately 100g.
Dry the fresh leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air-drying method for 7-10 days.
Combine dried melissa officinalis leaves with carrier oils such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil in a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Infuse the mixture using a double boiler or a water bath at 60°c for 2 hours allowing the ingredients to meld together.
Strain and filter the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter discarding any solids and transfer to sterilized glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley creams helps with pimples because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in parsley cream work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antibacterial agents combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that clog pores and cause breakouts. Additionally, parsley's antioxidant flavonoids help soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Parsley contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, potentially reducing inflammation and acne.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Parsley has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Antibacterial effects: Some research suggests that parsley extracts may have antibacterial properties, which could help combat bacteria like P. acnes that can cause acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, parsley roots help reduce inflammation and prevent further breakouts.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, parsley seeds soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh petroselinum crispum leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine the leaves with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to allow for infusion and decarboxylation of the herb.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before whipping the cream with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 5 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with pimples because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that effectively combat acne-causing bacteria. The natural astringent qualities of sweet briar also help to reduce sebum production, unclog pores, and soothe redness and inflammation. Additionally, the creams' antioxidant properties protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy cell turnover, allowing for faster healing and reduced appearance of pimples. This multi-faceted approach makes sweet briar creams an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and Antibacterial Properties: The antibacterial compounds present in Sweet Briar (Epilobium angustifolium) help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause acne, promoting a healthy skin environment.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids found in Sweet Briar cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples.

Wound-Healing and Tissue Repair: The astringent and tannin-rich compounds in Sweet Briar cream can help promote wound healing by constricting blood vessels, reducing inflammation, and accelerating the repair of damaged tissues, which can aid in acne scar treatment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with acne.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent bacterial growth and reduce acne-causing infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosa rubiginosa petals from a reputable supplier.
Combine harvested petals with other herbs and essential oils in a mortar and pestle.
Infuse the herbal mixture in a carrier oil for several hours or overnight.
Filter and decant the infused oil into clean glass jars.
Package and store the final product in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The herb's antibacterial compounds, such as camphor and salvenic acid, also combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to breakouts. Additionally, sage creams' astringent and antiseptic qualities help dry out pimples and prevent new ones from forming. Overall, sage creams provide a natural and effective solution for controlling acne and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds help kill bacteria that cause acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, while also soothing and calming the skin.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, helping to protect the skin from environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin, reducing redness and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant have antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to acne, making it a effective ingredient in medicinal creams for pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them properly for at least two hours using shade.
Grind 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about five minutes.
Mix the ground salvia officinalis with 10 milliliters of coconut oil and 5 milliliters of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture continuously until it reaches a smooth consistency, then remove from heat and let cool for one hour at room temperature.
Pour the cooled cream into an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator for up to six months or use within three days after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, thereby preventing the formation of new pimples. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of thyme creams help to reduce redness and swelling associated with existing pimples, promoting a smoother and clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can cause inflammation and infection in acne-prone areas.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in thyme have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with pimples, making them less noticeable and more manageable.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne formation, helping to protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote healthy, clear skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm inflammation associated with pimples.
Barks: Thyme barks contain compounds that have antibacterial properties, making them effective against acne-causing bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).
remedy_recipe: Gather thymus vulgaris essential oil, beeswax, coconut oil, and distilled water.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Mix the melted beeswax with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:5 and heat for 10 minutes.
Add thymus vulgaris essential oil to the mixture at a dosage of 2% of the total weight and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify within 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with pimples because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne-prone skin. Witch hazel also has astringent properties that help to dry up excess oil and shrink the appearance of pores, allowing the skin to breathe and recover from breakouts. As a result, American witch hazel creams provide a natural and effective way to combat pimples and maintain healthy, clear-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, as well as protect the skin from further damage.

Glycosides: Glycosides, particularly those like hamamelitannin found in Witch Hazel, have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat the underlying causes of acne, such as P. acnes bacteria and fungal infections.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids, including berberine and sanguinarine found in Witch Hazel, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the appearance of pimples by killing off bacteria and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of tannins, which help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin.
Barks: The barks have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating acne-causing bacteria and preventing further infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and twigs for infusion.
Steep the infused mixture in boiling water for 20 minutes.
Combine the cooled infused liquid with beeswax and coconut oil.
Add aloe vera gel and tea tree essential oil to the melted mixture.
Pour the final mixture into clean containers for cooling and hardening.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for pimples are topical treatments made from plant extracts that help alleviate acne and promote healthy skin. They work by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and soothing irritated skin. Some of the best herbal creams for pimples include Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Silybum marianum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Lavandula angustifolia, and Saponaria officinalis. If left untreated, pimples can cause emotional distress, low self-esteem, and even affect daily life, relationships, and professional opportunities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pimples that are treated with herbal creams are blackheads, whiteheads, and other forms of acne. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem can help reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial growth, and unclog pores, effectively treating these skin issues. These natural remedies are often gentler on the skin than chemical-based products and may be suitable for sensitive skin or those who prefer alternative treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with pimples is a blend of aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile. Aloe vera soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and redness. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that combat acne-causing bacteria, while chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and promote healing. When applied together, these herbal creams create a powerful and natural remedy for pimples, promoting healthy skin and minimizing the risk of scarring. They work in harmony to heal and protect the skin.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, herbal creams can be an effective treatment for pimples due to their ability to reduce both inflammatory and noninflammatory acne lesions. The study, which analyzed 34 clinical trials involving 1753 participants, found that most trials showed a significant reduction in acne lesions and a relevant effect on acne severity after using herbal medicine. By addressing the underlying causes of acne, such as inflammation and sebum production, herbal creams can help to alleviate pimple symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals with mild to moderate acne vulgaris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. It also has the ability to soothe and calm the skin, creating an environment that prevents bacterial growth and promotes healing. As a result, calendula creams can help to reduce the appearance and severity of pimples, making them an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance and severity of pimples.
Saponins: These glycosides have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent bacterial overgrowth and reduce the risk of infection that can contribute to the development of pimples.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: They have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial infections that can lead to pimples.
Seeds: They contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and acne scars.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in full bloom for 2-3 weeks with scissors or pinch off individual blooms.
Dry the fresh flowers in a single layer on paper bags or a clean cloth for 1-2 weeks.
Create a calendula officinalis infused oil by mixing 1 part dried flowers with 5 parts carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix 1 part of the calendula officinalis infused oil with 2 parts of a cream base such as shea butter or coconut oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial properties, which effectively combat acne-causing bacteria. The tea tree oil present in these creams has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples. By controlling the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia creams promote the healing of pimples, allowing the skin to recover faster. This results in a smoother, clearer complexion and reduced likelihood of future breakouts. It's a natural solution for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This phenolic compound helps reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections, which are often the root cause of pimples.
Limonene: A terpene with antimicrobial properties, limonene aids in controlling bacterial growth, thereby preventing pimples from worsening.
Linalool: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, linalool helps soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and promoting healing of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acne and soothe skin irritations.
Seeds: Used because they contain a higher concentration of tea tree oil than the leaves, making them a valuable ingredient in skincare products.
Flowers: Used for their gentle and soothing properties, which can help calm and reduce inflammation associated with pimples and acne.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 750ml of carrier oil such as jojoba oil or sweet almond oil for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the leaves and any remaining plant material.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter with the infused oil in a heat-proof container to create a smooth blend.
Add 10ml of vitamin e oil and 2ml of preservative to the blend and stir well to ensure everything is fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with pimples because they contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe and calm the skin. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples. Additionally, aloe vera's moisturizing and regenerative properties promote healthy skin cell growth, reducing the appearance of acne scars and promoting a smoother, clearer complexion. This makes aloe vera cream an effective natural remedy for acne and skin problems.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce redness and prevent infections that can cause pimples.
Aloin: A glycoside with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, aloin helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing scarring from pimples.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to the formation of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and calm acne-prone skin.
Gel from Leaves: Used for its ability to hydrate and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of pimples and acne.
Juice from Leaves: Used for its antibacterial properties, which help kill bacteria that cause pimples and acne, while also soothing and calming the skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100g of aloe barbadensis gel and 20g of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Weigh 20g of shea butter and 10g of coconut oil in a separate heat-proof container.
Melt the beeswax mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes with constant stirring.
Combine the melted beeswax mixture with the aloe barbadensis gel and mix until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to an airtight container and refrigerate at 40°f for 30 minutes to solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with pimples because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like vitamin E and beta-carotene that promote skin healing and reduce redness. Aloe vera creams also have antibacterial properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria, thereby preventing the spread of pimples. Additionally, its moisturizing properties help to regulate oil production, reducing the occurrence of clogged pores and pimples. This makes aloe vera creams an effective natural remedy for acne and pimple treatment.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.

Vitamins and minerals (including vitamin A and vitamin E): These nutrients promote skin cell turnover, reduce oxidative stress, and prevent collagen degradation, which can help prevent clogged pores and reduce pimple formation.

Polysaccharides (including acemannan): These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting faster healing of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and reduce acne.
Gel from the Leaves: This gel is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which has antioxidant properties that help to calm and protect the skin from acne and other skin irritations.

Aloe Vera Extract from the Leaves: This extract is often used in creams due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, making it effective in treating acne and other skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of aloe vera gel and 1/2 cup of distilled water to start making herbal aloe vera cream.
Mix 1 tablespoon of glycerin and 2 tablespoons of beeswax into the aloe vera gel and water mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 120-140 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1/2 teaspoon of chamomile essential oil to the cooled mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and refrigerate for 2-3 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work to reduce redness and swelling, allowing pimples to heal faster. Additionally, Echinacea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria, Echinacea creams can help alleviate pimple symptoms and promote healthy skin, providing a natural and effective solution for those struggling with acne.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of acne by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and other mediators.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help prevent bacterial infections that can cause acne and reduce the severity of existing pimples.

Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and oxidative stress associated with acne, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Roots: They are used because of their potential to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause acne and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: They are used for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce acne and prevent future breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh echinacea purpurea roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to reduce moisture.
Grind 1 ounce of dried echinacea purpurea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 ounce of echinacea powder with 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth and consistent texture and cools to room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with pimples because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, and also prevent bacterial growth that can cause breakouts. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin also helps to reduce the appearance of pimples, promoting a clearer and more even-toned complexion. As a result, Zingiber officinale creams can be an effective natural solution for managing acne and pimples.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Shogaols: Shogaols have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing acne, thereby preventing the formation of pimples.
Zingerone: This compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the skin from damage and reduce the severity of pimples.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale in skincare products, including creams for pimples, due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make creams for pimples, as they contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and reduce inflammation.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used in skincare products, including creams for pimples, as they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce redness and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried zingiber officinale roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids then measure out 2 ounces of the liquid extract.
Mix the extract with 8 ounces of distilled water and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat then stir occasionally for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 1 hour before whipping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with pimples because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that reduces inflammation and fights bacteria that cause acne. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples. Additionally, silymarin has been shown to improve skin elasticity and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, making it an effective treatment for various skin issues, including acne and pimples. This results in a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Silymarin: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the severity of pimples.
Linoleic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of pimples, while also regulating sebum production to prevent future breakouts.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of silymarin, a flavonoid compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to regulate skin inflammation, reduce redness, and promote healing, making them suitable for treating pimples.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their potential to reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation, while also promoting skin regeneration and collagen production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in large quantities to ensure sufficient raw material for the cream.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum plant parts in a warm well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Extract the active compounds from the dried silybum marianum plant parts using a solvent like ethanol in a 1:2 ratio for 2 hours.
Mix the extracted compound with a carrier oil like coconut oil in a 1:3 ratio to create a smooth paste.
Sterilize and fill the mixture into clean containers for a shelf life of up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin also helps to reduce inflammation, allowing pimples to heal faster. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, making it an effective natural remedy for pimples and acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of acne by neutralizing free radicals that can damage skin cells.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce redness and prevent bacterial infections that can cause pimples.
Liquiritin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and reduce pimple inflammation.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these compounds help to combat bacterial growth and reduce acne-causing inflammation.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and containing triterpenoid saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and crush them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 100g of glycerin and 50g of distilled water in a saucepan and heat them gently over medium heat.
Add the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra to the glycerin mixture and stir constantly for 10 minutes to prevent lumps.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture cool to room temperature for 30 minutes before straining.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax with the cooled mixture in a double boiler and heat until melted.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with pimples because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that reduce redness and prevent bacterial growth. The calming and soothing effects of lavender oil also help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can contribute to acne. Additionally, lavender's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote the healing of pimples, reducing their appearance and duration. This makes Lavandula angustifolia creams a popular natural remedy for acne and pimples.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anti-inflammatory to reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antimicrobial properties to prevent bacterial overgrowth that can cause acne and pimples.
Cineole: Displays antioxidant properties to reduce oxidative stress and promote skin healing, helping to alleviate pimple inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Leaves: They are used because of their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which aid in reducing acne and preventing further breakouts.
Stems: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discard the solids.
Combine the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir until the beeswax and shea butter have melted completely.
Pour the hot mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis creams helps with pimples because they contain natural ingredients that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's saponins and other compounds help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples. This promotes a faster healing process and reduces the likelihood of further breakouts. Additionally, Saponaria officinalis creams often contain soothing and calming agents that help to reduce stress on the skin, leading to a more even and balanced complexion. This natural remedy can provide a gentle yet effective solution for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help with pimples by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, which can contribute to acne.
Flavonoids: Help with pimples by exhibiting antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the immune response to prevent inflammation.
Saponins: Help with pimples by inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that can contribute to acne, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Roots: Contain saponins, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves on a sunny day around mid-morning.
Mix 2 tablespoons of dried saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves with 1 cup of olive oil in a bowl.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours in a warm place away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate bowl to remove the plant material after 2 hours.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the strained mixture and heat it gently.
